message:
feat(simulation): Cx-first execute_until + execute_all (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Expand Cx-first surface into simulation.rs. `Scenario::execute_until`
drives an event-injection loop on a MockWezterm; a long scenario
can't be cancelled mid-playback without cx threading.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `Scenario::execute_until_with_cx(cx, mock, elapsed)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `execute_until`. Checkpoints at 2 seams:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns `Ok(0)` before any
        injection (actually returns Err with the cancelled reason
        — corrected: the pre-flight check uses `map_err` to wrap
        into Error::Runtime, so a pre-cancelled cx surfaces as
        Err rather than silently returning 0).
      - per-event: `cx.checkpoint()?` at the top of each loop
        iteration, embedding the current event index so the
        caller knows how far the scenario progressed.

  * `Scenario::execute_all_with_cx(cx, mock)` — pure delegate to
    `execute_until_with_cx` with the scenario's full duration,
    mirroring the legacy `execute_all` → `execute_until`
    delegation.

Cancellation-safety contract: the checkpoint fires BEFORE each
`mock.inject` call, so a mid-run cancel leaves the mock in a
well-defined state (all previous events fully applied, no
partial inject). The scenario's `at > elapsed` termination
condition is checked BEFORE the checkpoint so the normal
termination path is unchanged.

Regression test (new) `execute_all_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Loads the same `BASIC_SCENARIO` YAML into two separate
    MockWezterm instances, runs `execute_all` on one and
    `execute_all_with_cx` on the other with a fresh
    `Cx::for_request()`.
  * Asserts:
    - event counts match (both return 2).
    - pane text output is bit-for-bit identical across legacy
      and Cx paths.
    - expected content ("hello world", "done") appears in both.

The text-parity assertion is the strongest regression guard: if
the Cx-first loop accidentally skipped an event, reordered
injections, or injected into the wrong pane, the text output
would diverge.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * simulation:: — 183 passed / 0 failed (tick 64: 182 -> tick 65:
    183, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (32.33s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
